raw_response:
I agree with the assessment provided by @Agent2 regarding Option B as it aligns well with established knowledge concerning enterococcal behavior post-endodontic therapy. One additional point supporting E. faecalis's role includes studies showing its high prevalence even after root canal treatments where irrigants were used effectively against many other microorganisms; this underscores how uniquely suited it may be to evade typical therapeutic measures.

Answer Choice: B Yes!
